basmala: In the Name of God, the Infinitely Merciful, the Most Merciful.
intro: Al-Naṣr — “Divine Help”, also known as “the Farewell Sūra” (al-Tawdīʿ, التَّوْدِيع) — is among the last sūras revealed, most probably the last to be revealed in its entirety. Tradition places it at the end of the Prophet's life, around the Farewell Pilgrimage in year 10 of the Hijra. Ibn ʿAbbās reports that on hearing it, the Prophet understood that his death was near: when God's help and victory have come, the time of the mission is ending and the time of return is opening. Three short verses, but they say at once the historical victory of Islam and the inward posture it asks for: not the pride of success, but praise and the asking of forgiveness.

versets.trad: When God's help and victory come,
versets.commentaire: naṣr Allāh (نَصْر اللَّه), the help of God, is not a victory won by a man's own strength, but the divine support that makes effort bear fruit. al-fatḥ (الفَتْح), “the opening”, historically names the conquest of Mecca in year 8 — not a seizure by force, but an “opening” that made the return of hearts possible. Mystical commentators also hear al-fatḥ as “the opening of the gates of arrival, the unveiling of the lights of Beauty and Majesty” (al-Baqlī). Victory, in the Quran, is first of all a gift.

mots.def: temporal particle: when, at the moment that.
jāʾa: to come, to arrive, to befall.
naṣr: help, assistance that makes victory possible; the word implies support coming from elsewhere.
Allāh: God; here in the genitive: “of God”.
coordinating conjunction: and.
fatḥ: opening, breakthrough; here the conquest (the “opening” of Mecca), but also every spiritual opening. Root f-t-ḥ: to open.

versets.ar: وَرَأَيْتَ النَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ أَفْوَاجًا
versets.tr: wa-raʾayta n-nāsa yadkhulūna fī dīni Llāhi afwājā
versets.trad: and you see mankind entering the Religion of God in throngs,
versets.commentaire: afwājan (أَفْوَاجًا) — in throngs, in waves. Before the opening of Mecca, people entered Islam one by one, or in small numbers; afterwards the tribes came in masses. The verse describes a precise historical moment, but also a wider law: there come hours when the heart of the world opens, and what seemed difficult becomes self-evident.

mots.def: conjunction: and.
raʾā: to see; here in the perfect with a present sense in the conditional.
an-nās: mankind, the people, humanity.
dakhala: to enter; here in the imperfect plural: they enter.
preposition: in, into.
dīn: religion, way, due order; it names at once the practice and the debt owed to God.
Allāh: God; here in the genitive.
fawj: a troop, a band; in the plural afwāj, “in successive bands”, “in waves”.

versets.ar: فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَاسْتَغْفِرْهُ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ تَوَّابًا
versets.tr: fa-sabbiḥ bi-ḥamdi rabbika wa-staghfirh, innahu kāna tawwābā
versets.trad: hymn the praises of your Lord and ask forgiveness of Him. He, truly, welcomes every repentance.
versets.commentaire: In the hour of success the command is not to rejoice, but to glorify: sabbiḥ (سَبِّحْ), “proclaim the transcendence”, underlines that God is beyond anything one could ascribe to Him. And to ask for forgiveness — istaghfir (اسْتَغْفِرْ) — even when no apparent fault has been committed: forgiveness becomes a posture, the recognition that nothing is ever level with the Gift received. Tawwāb (تَوَّاب) names the One who turns back to man again and again; in Arabic, the same word, tawba (تَوْبَة), names both man's repentance and God's relenting — two movements that answer each other.

mots.def: linking particle: then, so; introduces the consequence.
sabbaḥa: to declare God free of all imperfection, to proclaim His transcendence; imperative.
ḥamd: praise; preposition bi- + noun in the genitive: “with the praise of”, “in praise of”.
rabb: the Lord; with suffix pronoun 2nd pers. masc. sing.: your Lord.
conjunction: and.
istaghfara: to ask forgiveness, to ask for the veiling of faults; imperative with suffix pronoun: “ask Him for forgiveness”.
mots.gram: imperative + suffix pronoun, 2nd pers. masc. sing.

mots.def: emphasis particle inna + suffix pronoun 3rd pers. masc. sing.: “truly He”.

mots.sens: He is, He has always been
mots.def: kāna: to be; in the perfect, names a lasting state.
tawwāb: intensive form of tāʾib, “the One who turns back continually”; divine Name: the One who welcomes repentance and returns to it. The same word, tawba, also names man's repentance.
